reasoning: The Lean signature captures the informal statement precisely. `{G : Type*} [Group G]` matches "G is a group"; `(H K : Subgroup G)` matches "H and K are subgroups of G"; `[Fintype H] [Fintype K]` matches "finite subgroups"; `Nat.Coprime (card H) (card K)` (with `open Fintype` in scope, so `card` is `Fintype.card`, i.e., the order of each subgroup) matches "whose orders are relatively prime"; and `H ⊓ K = ⊥` matches "H ∩ K = 1" (the trivial subgroup in Mathlib is ⊥, and subgroup intersection is ⊓). No extra hypotheses are introduced and none are dropped. This matches the existing Mathlib lemma `Subgroup.inf_eq_bot_of_coprime`.
